hard_checks.description: Each stacked .sc has exactly one flex:1 absorber with min-height:0 and overflow:hidden
Slide CSS in <style> blocks contains no ad-hoc 6/3-digit hex literals outside the :root token block
Body contains no characters in the U+1F300–U+1FAFF emoji range
At 375×812 viewport, body.scrollWidth ≤ 375 AND every .g2/.g3/.flip-row/.tabs has flex-direction: column

Every .logo SVG renders with non-zero bounding box AND the brand-wm symbol contains either a real <path> with d>40 chars OR an <image href> attribute (PNG/SVG-as-image fallback). Pure <text>-only fallback is INSUFFICIENT.

Pixel-level visibility safeguard. Crops the cover screenshot to the logo's bounding rect, samples its pixels, compares against the cover background colour. Fails when ≥ 95 % of the logo region is within 30 RGB-Euclidean units of the background — i.e. the logo is technically embedded but rendered same-colour as the background and effectively invisible. The byte-level logo_renders check cannot see this; this check is the safeguard. Skipped (PASS, advisory) when PIL is unavailable or measurement data is missing.
无块级文本元素被截断（overflow:hidden 且 scrollHeight > clientHeight），无 slide 最低文本元素贴底（gap < 18px），H1/H2/H3 不超过 3 行；封面（slide 0）豁免
DS markdown contains all of: 'Single-Slide Fit Contract', 'three-layer overflow safety net', 'inline-flex trap', 'this.classList.toggle', '12 px floor', 'Typography Safety' (case-insensitive)

zh decks only: at least one CJK font (PingFang SC / Hiragino Sans GB / Microsoft YaHei / Source Han Sans SC) is in the body font-family chain. Hard FAIL only when zero CJK fonts present (CJK chars would fall through to OS thin-default). Order is a soft preference — vision judge decides if the rendered CJK looks good. Skipped for en decks.

Phase B governance gate. SHA-256 hashes the deck and DS each run; if a re-run shows the deck changed but the DS didn't, FAILS as 'deck_modified_without_ds_update'. Phase B forbids deck-only fixes — every check failure must be resolved by editing the brand DS section that owns the rule and regenerating the deck. First runs and clean re-runs both pass.

judge_dimensions.name: logo_present_and_branded
judge_dimensions.description: Is a recognisable BRAND logo visible on every slide (white-on-dark on cover; brand-dark on light slides)? Or is it a typographic placeholder / missing entirely?
judge_dimensions.scoring_guide: 5 = real brand wordmark/mark visible and clean on every slide; 4 = real logo but minor positioning/clarity issue; 3 = typographic placeholder (e.g. brand name set in regular text) — usable but obviously not the real logo; 2 = degenerate placeholder (e.g. just 'X' or generic disc) or invisible on dark; 1 = visibly broken (clipped/garbled); 0 = no logo at all

judge_dimensions.name: slide_visual_quality
judge_dimensions.description: Across the slides shown, is layout balanced, breathing-room visible at bottom, type hierarchy clear, no awkward gaps or cramped rows? Cover should feel commanding; content slides should look composed not crammed.
judge_dimensions.scoring_guide: 5 = every slide visually composed; 4 = 1-2 slides minor (slight imbalance); 3 = several slides feel awkward (cramped, weak hierarchy, mis-aligned); 2 = consistent layout problems; 1 = obviously broken; 0 = unusable

judge_dimensions.name: brand_fidelity
judge_dimensions.description: Does the deck's overall visual register match the source brand's known visual language (palette mood, type personality, decorative restraint)? Judge against your prior knowledge of the source brand.
judge_dimensions.scoring_guide: 5 = unmistakably on-brand; 4 = on-brand with minor liberties; 3 = recognisable but generic-feeling; 2 = generic AI/SaaS aesthetic, not brand-specific; 1 = wrong mood; 0 = no brand identity at all

judge_dimensions.name: content_substantive
judge_dimensions.description: Is the slide copy real, substantive, on-domain business writing? Not lorem ipsum, not 'discover the seamless future', not vague AI filler.
judge_dimensions.scoring_guide: 5 = every line earns its place, sounds like real internal business writing; 4 = mostly substantive, 1-2 lines vague; 3 = mixed (some real, some filler); 2 = mostly filler/generic; 1 = lorem-flavoured; 0 = empty/placeholder

judge_dimensions.name: engineering_dna_visible_in_ds
judge_dimensions.description: Does the Design System markdown explicitly carry the engineering invariants: §1 Constraints/Freedom, §5 Fit Contract incl. three-layer overflow + single absorber + 602px budget, §10 Mobile incl. inline-flex trap + flip-card mobile fix, §13 ~40-item Checklist incl. 375px verification?
judge_dimensions.scoring_guide: 5 = all sections present and verbatim from template; 4 = present but minor wording drift; 3 = key sections present but at least one diluted; 2 = major section missing; 1 = engineering DNA largely absent; 0 = template not followed

judge_dimensions.name: cjk_typography_quality
judge_dimensions.description: zh decks only (skip + return 5 for en decks). Looking at the rendered slide screenshots, do the CJK glyphs look high-quality and on-brand? Specifically: (a) Are CJK chars rendered with a characterful family (PingFang SC / Hiragino Sans GB / Source Han Sans / Songti SC), not the thin OS default? Thin-default looks visibly cheap — strokes too fine, weight too light, characters appear to float. (b) Do CJK weight and proportions match the deck's Latin typography? A 700-weight Latin headline next to 400-weight thin CJK = mismatch. (c) For serif Latin brands (Coca-Cola Georgia / editorial), is CJK using a serif family (Songti / Noto Serif), or does it clash with a sans CJK? (d) Does the overall feel match the brand's voice?
judge_dimensions.scoring_guide: 5 = CJK rendering is clearly characterful, weights match Latin, harmonious with brand; 4 = looks good with one minor mismatch; 3 = readable but feels generic — could be PingFang Regular when the deck wanted Medium/Bold weight; 2 = visibly thin / cheap — looks like OS thin-default; 1 = serif/sans clash with Latin AND thin CJK rendering; 0 = unreadable. If you score ≤3, the fix is in DS §3 CJK 字体回退链 — move CJK family to front of font-family chain, or bump body font-weight from 400 to 500/600.

disqualifiers.trigger: agent-browser eval reports any console.error during render
